raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'Check this' refer to (1) writing polish and grammar only, (2) logical consistency and evidence alignment, or (3) a comprehensive review including novelty and scholarship?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The vague delegation to 'check' a paper draft centrally expects verification of logical consistency in the argument chain.","R1.3":"Checking the draft involves ensuring claims align with reported evidence like BLEU scores and correlations.","R1.6":"Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is a central expected responsibility for reviewing a draft.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected when checking a research draft making empirical claims.","RX.2":"Avoidance of overclaims is a key cross-cutting responsibility in evaluating the thesis and findings.","RX.3":"Adherence to the stated scope of MT instruction-following evaluation is centrally relevant to the check.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is expected in reviewing a draft on LLM-based MT that could impact production deployments.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability of benchmarks like WMT and FLORES are central for a responsible check of the draft."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'Check this' refer to (1) writing polish and grammar only, (2) logical consistency and evidence alignment, or (3) a comprehensive review including novelty and scholarship?

rationale.R1.2: The vague delegation to 'check' a paper draft centrally expects verification of logical consistency in the argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Checking the draft involves ensuring claims align with reported evidence like BLEU scores and correlations.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish at the sentence and paragraph level is a central expected responsibility for reviewing a draft.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected when checking a research draft making empirical claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoidance of overclaims is a key cross-cutting responsibility in evaluating the thesis and findings.
rationale.RX.3: Adherence to the stated scope of MT instruction-following evaluation is centrally relevant to the check.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is expected in reviewing a draft on LLM-based MT that could impact production deployments.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability of benchmarks like WMT and FLORES are central for a responsible check of the draft.
